pi_items.source: Windows path format is correct for Docker container environment with Docker user account. File location was not found in workspace or documentation. File would need to be created by setup_task.ps1 hook (currently missing). CSV is expected to contain photoplethysmogram (PPG/PLETH) waveform data extracted or derived from VitalDB.

Windows path format is correct and consistent with file output paths used in other vital recorder tasks. File naming follows vital recorder naming convention (.vital extension). Path structure verified against env.json Docker user configuration.

Windows path format is correct and consistent with screenshot output paths used in other vital recorder tasks. Desktop folder is standard location for user-generated outputs. Path structure verified against env.json Docker user configuration.

Threshold value specified in task metadata. Actual vital files in environment are significantly larger: 0001.vital (21MB), 0002.vital (21MB), 0003.vital (6.3MB). File inspection confirms vital files are gzip-compressed binary format with uncompressed size typically 50-100MB. A 1024-byte minimum is reasonable for a valid vital file.

pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB is a real, open-access dataset containing genuine surgical patient vital signs including photoplethysmography (PPG/PLETH) waveforms
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed VitalDB is a peer-reviewed, publicly available dataset at https://vitaldb.net and https://physionet.org/content/vitaldb/1.0.0/. Nature Scientific Data publication (https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-022-01411-5) documents VitalDB containing high-fidelity multi-parameter vital signs from 6,388 surgical cases at Seoul National University Hospital. PPG signals recorded from fingertip at 500 Hz via GE Solar8000 monitor (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9178032/). Verified_pi.json files in same environment (annotate_hemodynamic_events, generate_downsampled_matlab) confirm PLETH is a standard track in VitalDB at 500 Hz sampling rate.

pi_items.verified_value: Vital file is gzip-compressed binary format with header (1F 8B 08 00) per VitalDB documentation at https://vitaldb.net/docs/?documentId=1S_orMIzhL9XlWDArqh3h4rAp_IRXJs8kL7n07vAJhU8. File inspection confirms gzip compression: 0002.vital is gzip compressed data with original size ~86.8 MB.

pi_items.source: Version 1.16.6 is specified in install_vitalrecorder.ps1 hook and is a documented release version of Vital Recorder (https://vitaldb.net/vital-recorder/). This version is confirmed available from https://vitaldb.net/getvr.php?type=msi&ver=1.16.6

pi_items.verified_value: Installed to C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\VitalRecorder\Vital.exe via MSI package at https://vitaldb.net/getvr.php?type=msi&ver=1.16.6
pi_items.source: Verified through install_vitalrecorder.ps1 script which downloads and installs VitalRecorder MSI with REINSTALL=ALL REINSTALLMODE=omus flags to force full file extraction

pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB contains 6,388 surgical cases with 486,451 waveform and numeric data tracks of 196 intraoperative monitoring parameters, 73 perioperative clinical parameters, and 34 time-series laboratory result parameters. Recording duration averages 3.2 ± 1.9 hours per case.
pi_items.source: VitalDB official documentation at https://physionet.org/content/vitaldb/1.0.0/ and Nature Scientific Data article (https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-022-01411-5)

pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB dataset is released under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0) license and is freely available to researchers
pi_items.source: VitalDB website (https://vitaldb.net/dataset/) and official documentation confirm free public access for research purposes

task.json references setup_task.ps1 hook at C:\workspace\tasks\import_external_csv_data\setup_task.ps1, but this file does not exist in the task directory. Glob search found no setup_task.ps1 file for import_external_csv_data task. Similar export_result.ps1 hook also referenced but not found.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real physiological data from VitalDB (Vital Signs DataBase), a legitimate, peer-reviewed, publicly available open dataset containing 6,388 surgical cases with high-resolution vital signs including photoplethysmography (PPG/PLETH) waveforms recorded at 500 Hz from actual patients at Seoul National University Hospital. The vital file paths and file formats are correctly specified for Windows 11 Docker environment. Vital Recorder version 1.16.6 is confirmed as a real, publicly available release. However, the setup and result export scripts referenced in task.json hooks (setup_task.ps1 and export_result.ps1) do not exist in the task directory, and the input CSV file (prototype_pleth.csv) location cannot be verified to exist.
